title: Implement deterministic label-resolution policy for reference dataset builds
description:
## Summary
Define and implement deterministic label resolution for reference dataset builds.

## Label priority
1. vetted/gold feedback
2. regular final feedback
3. score-result or imported example label

## Deliverables
- Resolve labels according to the priority order above.
- Skip unresolved items.
- Emit skipped counts and item IDs/reasons so the build result is auditable.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: deterministic label-resolution policy for reference dataset builds with explicit source priority, unresolved-item skipping, and skip reporting.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented deterministic label-resolution policy in reference dataset row generation (FeedbackItems._create_dataset_rows).

Policy now enforced in one strict path:
1) vetted/gold feedback label (vetted_feedback)
2) regular final feedback label (regular_final_feedback)
3) score-result/imported label (score_result_or_imported): initialAnswerValue, then deterministic item.metadata keys

Behavior changes:
- Unresolved items are skipped (not emitted as rows).
- Skip reporting is produced per build:
  - self.last_label_resolution_report
  - df.attrs['label_resolution_report']
  - includes skipped count + skipped feedback_item_ids.
- Row metadata now records label source under metadata.label_resolution.

Files changed:
- plexus/data/FeedbackItems.py
- plexus/data/test_feedback_items.py

Targeted validation:
- pytest -q plexus/data/test_feedback_items.py -k "reference_label_resolution or skips_unresolved or create_dataset_rows_with_data"
- Result: 4 passed.
